title: qPCR optimization log (fakeGFP vs ACT1)
description:
Optimizing the SYBR-based qPCR for fakeGFP expression. Reference: alex pcr_protocol 1.

Doc tracks Cq values per primer-anneal sweep so we lock in conditions before the full triplicate run.

entries.title: 2026-04-22: anneal temp sweep 56, 58, 60, 62 °C
entries.content:
fakeGFP-fwd/rev at 200 nM, cDNA 1:5. Cq means (n=2):
- 56 °C: 22.9
- 58 °C: 22.4
- 60 °C: 21.7 (sharp melt peak)
- 62 °C: 22.1

Locking in 60 °C anneal. Melt curve confirms single product.

entries.title: 2026-04-29: primer concentration check (100 vs 200 nM)
entries.content: 100 nM: Cq 22.1, lower fluorescence plateau. 200 nM: Cq 21.7, plateau ~2× higher. Sticking with 200 nM for the demo runs.

entries.title: 2026-05-06: reference gene comparison ACT1 vs PDA1
entries.content: ACT1 Cq spread across 8 wells: 21.6 to 21.9 (SD 0.10). PDA1 Cq spread: 24.1 to 24.7 (SD 0.22). ACT1 is the tighter reference, using it as the housekeeping baseline.

comments.author: mira
comments.text: Nice tight ACT1 spread (SD 0.10) — that's a keeper reference. Let's discuss the 200 nM primer choice in Friday meeting; curious whether 150 nM still gives the same plateau and saves reagent burn over the long screen runs.
